segments.text:
Diphtheria
CLS-N390 Vaccines: Science and Society

Corynebacterium diphtheria
Gram positive bacillus
Member of Corynebacterium genus
M ost species are found all over including skin, mucous membranes, and intestine
H armless to humans and not speciated

Transmission
Most often person-to-person – respiratory tract
Rare – transmission from skin lesions or article soiled with discharge from lesions (fomites)
Incubation period: 2 – 5 days (range of 1 – 10)
Attaches to lining of respiratory system
Weakness
Sore throat
Mild fever
Swollen glands in neck
R 0 = 1.7 – 4.3; approximately 82% vaccinated for herd immunity

Symptoms
Produces toxin – kills tissue in respiratory system
Within 2 – 3 days – dead tissue forms thick, gray coating that builds up on throat and nose
Known as pseudomembrane
Makes it hard to breathe and swallow
Rarely may infect skin – open sores or ulcers
Treatment requires antibiotic to kill bacteria, but immediate need is to neutralize toxin
Antitoxin available from CDC

Complications
May lead to death
1 in 10 treated will die
Up to 50% not treated will die
If toxin gets into blood  heart, nerve, and kidney damage

Vaccine
Intended to elicit anti-toxin antibodies
Does not target bacteria

Diphtheroid Vaccine Timeline
E arly 1900s
P rophylaxis toxin-antitoxin
1921
T oxoid developed
1930s
T oxoid became more widely used
1940s
I ncorporated with tetanus & pertussis

Characteristics of Vaccine
Toxoid produced by growing bacteria in liquid media
Efficacy – 95%
Immunity – approximately 10 years
Filtrate incubated with formaldehyde – toxin  toxoid
Adsorbed onto an aluminum salt
<12 months 4 doses; 12+ months – 3 doses;
booster @ 4-6 years; booster @ 11-12 years

Vaccine Options
DT – pediatric diphtheria toxoid and tetanus toxoid
Given when child has pertussis contraindication
DTaP – pediatric diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, and acellular pertussis
Vaccines have 3 -4 times the amount of diphtheria toxoid as adult vaccines
<7 years old
Td – adult diphtheria toxoid and tetanus toxoid
Provided as booster shot every 10 years
Given if childhood series is missed and pertussis contraindication
Tdap – adult diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, and acellular pertussis
Given when childhood series is missed before 7
>7 years old
DTaP- HepB -IPV – combination of pediatric diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, acellular pertussis, hepatitis B, poliovirus
DTaP-IPV-Hib – combination of pediatric diphtheria toxoid, tetanus toxoid, acellular pertussis, poliovirus, and Haemophilus influenzae type B
Other

2020 Cases – 10,107
95.5% of cases from:
Ethiopia – 5,267
India
Indonesia
Viet Nam
Yemen
US has not seen a case of respiratory diphtheria since 2004
